images.caption: Figure 1:SIA across three diverse tasks.Each panel compares three operating points: Baseline (first generation, no SIA), SIA-H (harness updates only), and SIA-W+H (harness + weight updates), on LawBench Top-1 accuracy, TriMul CUDA speedup, and scRNA-seq denoisingmse_norm. The dashed line marks the previous state-of-the-art. SIA-W+H strictly outperforms SIA-H on all three tasks.
Figure 4:LawBench results.Top-1 accuracy for Baseline, SIA-H (harness only), and SIA-W+H (harness + weight updates). Dashed line: prior state-of-the-art.
Figure 5:TriMul CUDA results.Speedup over baseline for Baseline, SIA-H (harness only), and SIA-W+H (harness + weight updates). Dashed line: prior state-of-the-art.
Figure 6:Denoising results.MSEnorm{}_{\text{norm}}for Baseline, SIA-H (harness only), and SIA-W+H (harness + weight updates). Dashed line: prior state-of-the-art.
